required_skills: Avionics system architecture design and integration
Embedded hardware design for safety-critical aerospace applications
ARINC 429, MIL-STD-1553, and SpaceWire communication bus protocols
DO-254 hardware design assurance for airborne electronic hardware
DO-178C software considerations for avionics hardware-software integration
FPGA design and digital logic implementation (VHDL or Verilog)
PCB design and schematic capture for high-reliability electronics
Signal integrity analysis and electromagnetic interference (EMI) mitigation
Failure mode and effects analysis (FMEA) and design assurance level (DAL) assessment
preferred_skills: Experience with space-grade components and radiation-hardened electronics
Knowledge of MIL-STD-461 EMC testing requirements
Familiarity with CAN bus or Ethernet ARINC 664 (AFDX) protocols
RF systems and antenna integration experience
Experience with real-time operating systems (RTOS) integration with avionics hardware
Power electronics and power distribution unit (PDU) design
Knowledge of RTCA/DO-160 environmental conditions and test procedures
responsibilities: Design and architect avionics hardware systems including flight computers, data acquisition units, and power distribution modules for aircraft or spacecraft
Develop FPGA-based digital logic designs in VHDL or Verilog for real-time sensor processing and communication bus management
Execute DO-254 design assurance activities including hardware design plans, verification plans, and certification liaison with civil aviation authorities
Perform signal integrity, power integrity, and EMC analysis to ensure avionics hardware meets MIL-STD-461 and DO-160 requirements
Integrate avionics subsystems across ARINC 429, MIL-STD-1553, and SpaceWire data buses, validating end-to-end data flow and latency
Conduct hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) testing using dSPACE or NI platforms to validate avionics behavior under simulated flight conditions
Collaborate with flight software engineers to define hardware-software interfaces and allocate processing resources across avionics platforms
Author hardware requirements specifications, interface control documents, and component qualification test plans for airborne electronics
Lead component obsolescence management and evaluate radiation-hardened or high-reliability part alternatives for long-duration missions
Support environmental qualification campaigns including thermal cycling, vibration, and radiation testing to verify avionics hardware robustness
